chapter_title.ar: بَابُ أَنَّهُ لَا يُؤَاخَذُ الْمُسْلِمُ بِمَا عَمِلَ فِي الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 205 – It is such that the Muslim would not be seized for what he did during the pre-Islamic period

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (11/384)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (11/384)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يُحْسِنُ فِي الْإِسْلَامِ أَ يُؤَاخَذُ بِمَا عَمِلَ فِي الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ فَقَالَ قَالَ النَّبِيُّ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) مَنْ أَحْسَنَ فِي الْإِسْلَامِ لَمْ يُؤَاخَذْ بِمَا عَمِلَ فِي الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ وَ مَنْ أَسَاءَ فِي الْإِسْلَامِ أُخِذَ بِالْأَوَّلِ وَ الْآخِرِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Al Qasim Bin Muhammad Al Jowhary, from Al Minqary, from Fuzayl Bin Ayaz who said,

‘I asked Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> about the man is good during Al-Islam, would he be seized for what he had done during the pre-Islamic period?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The Prophet<sup>saww</sup> said: ‘The one who is good during Al-Islam would not be Seized for what he had done during the pre-Islamic period; but the one who is evil during Al-Islam would be Seized for the former and the latter’.
en.sarwar: 2. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from al-Qasim ibn Muhammad al- Jawhari from al-Minqari from Fudayl ibn al-‘Iyad who has said the following: “Once I asked abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) ‘Will a man who is good in Islam be held responsible for his deeds in the time of ignorance?’ The Imam said, ‘The Holy Prophet has said, “Whoever is good in Islam will not be held responsible for his deeds in the times of ignorance and anyone who is not good in Islam will be held responsible for his acts of the past and those thereafter.’”
